japanese: 麻婆ラーメン スペシャルトッピング

food_subgroup: Mixed protein ramen toppings (chashu pork, chicken chashu, braised pork, flavoured egg)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite ramen toppings set. FOODON_03315595 (pork food product) is used as the closest parent given that pork-based items (braised pork, pork chashu) are the dominant protein components. Chicken chashu and flavoured egg are not individually represented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Meat products and preparations) is the closest defensible parent for this composite toppings set. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for a mixed ramen toppings combination.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a composite ramen toppings set. SNOMED CT is not used for this record.

serving.description: 1 toppings set (4 items: 1 chicken chashu, 1 braised pork, 1 pork chashu, 1 flavoured egg)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving consists of four ramen toppings: one piece of chicken char siew, one portion of braised pork, one piece of pork char siew, and one flavoured soft-boiled egg (ajitsuke tamago), totalling approximately 200 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pork, cured, ham, extra lean (approximately 5% fat), canned, roasted; Chicken, broilers or fryers, breast, meat only, cooked, roasted; Egg, whole, cooked, hard-boiled; Pork, fresh, loin, whole, separable lean and fat, cooked, braised — composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite toppings set. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for: (1) chicken breast, roasted (proxy for chicken chashu); (2) pork loin, braised (proxy for braised pork and pork chashu); (3) hard-boiled egg (proxy for flavoured egg). Char siew preparation adds sugar and soy sauce; sodium is adjusted upward to reflect marination. Approximate component weights: chicken chashu ~40 g, braised pork ~50 g, pork chashu ~50 g, flavoured egg ~60 g, total ~200 g. Per-100g values derived from this weighted blend.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; analogous estimate based on protein-dominant mixed meat and egg dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite ramen toppings set. GI is estimated at 32 based on analogous protein-dominant mixed meat and egg dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). The dish is predominantly protein and fat with minimal carbohydrate (4.2 g per 100g, primarily from soy-based marinade sugars). Glycemic load per 200g serving = (32 x 8.4g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 2.7, classified as low GL. GI category is low, consistent with high-protein, low-carbohydrate mixed meat dishes.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy-based marinades and char siew preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this toppings set as the estimated sodium content of approximately 680 mg per 100g (1360 mg per serving) approaches or exceeds the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; portion reduction and requesting low-sodium preparation are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content with high protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: This toppings set has a low glycemic index (estimated 32) and low glycemic load per serving (2.7), making it a suitable protein-rich addition for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate ramen broth or noodles; however, the sugar content from char siew marinade should be noted.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from pork and egg components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of pork chashu, braised pork, and flavoured egg contributes approximately 3.8 g saturated fat and 145 mg cholesterol per 100g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate portion size and frequency of consumption in line with dietary saturated fat guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mapo Ramen Special Toppings is a premium ramen topping set served at Ramen Matsuri in Singapore, consisting of four distinct protein components: one piece of chicken char siew (Japanese-style roasted chicken with soy-mirin glaze), one portion of braised pork (slow-cooked in soy sauce and aromatics until tender), one piece of pork char siew (Japanese-style roasted pork belly or shoulder with caramelised soy-mirin crust), and one flavoured soft-boiled egg (ajitsuke tamago, marinated in soy sauce, mirin, and sake). The toppings are prepared using traditional Japanese ramen techniques adapted for the Singapore market. Chicken and pork char siew are roasted at high heat to develop a caramelised exterior while retaining moisture, then sliced and served atop ramen. Braised pork is slow-cooked until fork-tender with a rich umami flavour. The flavoured egg is soft-boiled to a jammy yolk consistency and marinated overnight in a soy-based tare. Nutritionally, this toppings set is protein-dense (approximately 18.5 g protein per 100g) and moderate in fat (11 g per 100g), with a low glycemic index (estimated 32) due to minimal carbohydrate content. Sodium is elevated (approximately 680 mg per 100g) due to soy-based marinades, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the low glycemic load (2.7 per serving) makes this a suitable protein addition. Cardiovascular disease patients should moderate intake due to saturated fat and cholesterol from pork and egg. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following halal dietary requirements due to pork content.
common_user_queries: What is in Mapo Ramen Special Toppings?
Is Mapo Ramen Special Toppings halal?
How many calories in ramen special toppings?
Is ramen toppings set good for diabetics?
Is ramen toppings high in sodium?
Can I eat ramen toppings if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite ramen toppings set. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging of USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the four constituent components (chicken chashu, braised pork, pork chashu, flavoured egg). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for protein-dominant mixed meat and egg dishes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. This is a composite toppings set with no direct entry in any approved primary source. All nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data. Sodium is adjusted upward from base USDA values to reflect soy-based marination typical of Japanese char siew and braised pork preparation. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and are not directly measured for this specific dish. 3. GI is estimated at 32 based on analogous protein-dominant mixed meat and egg dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this dish. 4. The dish contains pork and is not halal. 5. Serving size of 200g is an estimate based on typical ramen toppings set weights; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 6. dietary_fibre_g of 0.1 g per 100g reflects trace fibre from marinade ingredients (garlic, ginger); this is a near-zero value consistent with a predominantly meat and egg dish. 7. sugar_g of 2.8 g per 100g reflects added sugars from mirin and sugar used in char siew and braised pork marinades.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (32), gi_category (low), glycemic_load_per_serving (2.7), and gi_source are all present. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for protein-dominant mixed meat and egg dishes, with estimation basis fully documented.
